root_text.an5.174:1.1: Atha kho anāthapiṇḍiko gahapati yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho anāthapiṇḍikaṁ gahapatiṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an5.174:2.1: “Pañca, gahapati, bhayāni verāni appahāya ‘dussīlo’ iti vuccati, nirayañca upapajjati.
root_text.an5.174:2.4: imāni kho, gahapati, pañca bhayāni verāni appahāya ‘dussīlo’ iti vuccati, nirayañca upapajjati.
root_text.an5.174:3.1: Pañca, gahapati, bhayāni verāni pahāya ‘sīlavā’ iti vuccati, sugatiñca upapajjati.
root_text.an5.174:3.4: imāni kho, gahapati, pañca bhayāni verāni pahāya ‘sīlavā’ iti vuccati, sugatiñca upapajjati.
root_text.an5.174:4.1: Yaṁ, gahapati, pāṇātipātī pāṇātipātapaccayā diṭṭhadhammikampi bhayaṁ veraṁ pasavati, samparāyikampi bhayaṁ veraṁ pasavati, cetasikampi dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti, pāṇātipātā paṭivirato neva diṭṭhadhammikaṁ bhayaṁ veraṁ pasavati, na samparāyikaṁ bhayaṁ veraṁ pasavati, na cetasikaṁ dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti.
root_text.an5.174:4.2: Pāṇātipātā paṭiviratassa evaṁ taṁ bhayaṁ veraṁ vūpasantaṁ hoti.
root_text.an5.174:8.1: Yaṁ, gahapati, surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhāyī surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhānapaccayā diṭṭhadhammikampi bhayaṁ veraṁ pasavati, samparāyikampi bhayaṁ veraṁ pasavati, cetasikampi dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti, surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhānā paṭivirato neva diṭṭhadhammikaṁ bhayaṁ veraṁ pasavati, na samparāyikaṁ bhayaṁ veraṁ pasavati, na cetasikaṁ dukkhaṁ domanassaṁ paṭisaṁvedeti.
root_text.an5.174:8.2: Surāmerayamajjapamādaṭṭhānā paṭiviratassa evaṁ taṁ bhayaṁ veraṁ vūpasantaṁ hotīti.

translation_text.an5.174:1.1: Then the householder Anāthapiṇḍika went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him:
translation_text.an5.174:2.1: “Householder, unless these five fears and enmities are given up, one is said to be unethical, and is reborn in hell.
translation_text.an5.174:2.3: Killing living creatures, stealing, committing sexual misconduct, lying, and consuming beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants.
translation_text.an5.174:2.4: Unless these five fears and enmities are given up, one is said to be unethical, and is reborn in hell.
translation_text.an5.174:3.1: Once these five fears and enmities are given up, one is said to be ethical, and is reborn in heaven.
translation_text.an5.174:3.3: Killing living creatures, stealing, committing sexual misconduct, lying, and consuming beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants.
translation_text.an5.174:3.4: Once these five fears and enmities are given up, one is said to be ethical, and is reborn in heaven.
translation_text.an5.174:4.1: Anyone who kills living creatures brims with fear and enmity both in this life and in lives to come, and experiences mental pain and sadness. Anyone who refrains from killing living creatures does not brim with fear and enmity either in this life or in lives to come, and doesn’t experience mental pain and sadness.
translation_text.an5.174:4.2: So that fear and enmity is quelled for anyone who refrains from killing living creatures.
translation_text.an5.174:8.1: Anyone who consumes beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants brims with fear and enmity both in this life and in lives to come, and experiences mental pain and sadness. Anyone who refrains from consuming beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants does not brim with fear and enmity either in this life or in lives to come, and doesn’t experience mental pain and sadness.
translation_text.an5.174:8.2: So that fear and enmity is quelled for anyone who refrains from consuming beer, wine, and liquor intoxicants.
translation_text.an5.174:10.1: Unless they give up these five threats,
translation_text.an5.174:10.3: When their body breaks up, that witless person
translation_text.an5.174:11.1: A person in the world doesn’t kill living creatures,
translation_text.an5.174:12.3: When their body breaks up, that wise person
translation_text.an5.174:12.4: is reborn in a good place.”
